prerequisites: An Atlassian Cloud account with access to the target Confluence site.
The Confluence Cloud domain name, taken from your site URL https://<domain_name>.atlassian.net/wiki (provide it as the domain_name config field).
The Atlassian account email address associated with that account (provide it as the email config field).
An Atlassian API token created for that account (provide it as the api_token config field).

authMethods.name: Basic auth (email + API token)
authMethods.summary: The Confluence Cloud REST API supports HTTP Basic authentication. Authenticate by pairing your Atlassian account email with an API token. The connector maps to this with the email and api_token fields: it forms the credentials string email:api_token, base64-encodes it, and sends it as the Authorization: Basic header.

setupSteps.title: Create an Atlassian API token
setupSteps.body: Sign in to your Atlassian account and go to https://id.atlassian.com/manage/api-tokens. Create a new API token, give it a label, and copy the generated value. You cannot view the token again after closing the dialog, so store it securely.

setupSteps.title: Identify your Confluence domain and account email
setupSteps.body: Your Confluence Cloud site URL has the form https://<domain_name>.atlassian.net/wiki. Use the <domain_name> portion for the domain_name field. Use the Atlassian account email that owns the API token for the email field.

setupSteps.title: Configure the connector credentials
setupSteps.body: Set domain_name to your Confluence domain, email to your Atlassian account email, and api_token to the token you created. The connector uses these to call the Confluence Cloud REST API base URL https://<domain_name>.atlassian.net/wiki with Basic auth (email:api_token, base64-encoded in the Authorization header).
